user_feedback: Liked v12 submerged shark direction, but background felt weird.
rationale: Cleaner background than v12: removes the odd cracked foreground/tile pattern and stacked aquarium-shelf feeling.
Shark reads as in the water/underwater, not sitting on top.
Barren coast/rocky lifeless land remains visible enough to support the before-trees idea.
No obvious trees, plants, grass, or forest elements.
Single calm shark and premium natural-history frame quality.

runner_ups.v15_05: cleanest simple underwater look with one shark, but less clear as coastline/before-trees setting
runner_ups.v15_02: premium and clean, but canyon walls dominate and ancient-coast idea is less obvious

rejected_attempts.v13: simplified guide caused aquarium/display-box compositions, extra sharks, or plant-like/coral forms
rejected_attempts.v14: img2img preserved v12 shark but also preserved the weird cracked foreground/background issue

next_if_refining: Use v15_03 as target; try to make the shark slightly larger and keep exactly one shark, while preserving the clean distant barren coastline.
